@@ -0,0 +1,243 @@
+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-94
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-22_20-41-02+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs — new opt-in adapter module + 7 unit tests
+- crates/io-gltf/src/lib.rs — `mod asset_store_cache;`, `pub use AssetStoreCache`, new `GltfError::Cache(String)` variant
+- crates/io-gltf/src/cache_stub.rs — comment-only doctrine update (no code changes)
+- crates/io-gltf/Cargo.toml — minimal direct path dep on `rge-asset-store`
+- Cargo.lock — single edge added: `rge-io-gltf -> rge-asset-store`
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-94_TASK_2026-05-22_20-20-17+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `crates/io-gltf/Cargo.toml`: added `rge-asset-store = { path = "../asset-store" }`
+  in `[dependencies]` (with an ISSUE-94 rationale comment). No edit to root
+  `Cargo.toml` workspace deps.
+- `crates/io-gltf/src/lib.rs`:
+  - Added `mod asset_store_cache;` and `pub use asset_store_cache::AssetStoreCache;`
+    alongside the existing `pub use cache_stub::{Cache, MemoryCache};` (unchanged).
+  - Updated the W17 stub-policy comment over the `mod cache_stub;` line to note
+    that asset-store is now reached through the opt-in adapter rather than by
+    replacing the stub.
+  - Added a new `GltfError::Cache(String)` variant gated by an `#[error("cache: {0}")]`
+    message; existing `Io` / `Parse` / `Schema` / `Json` variants and their
+    `From` impls are unchanged.
+- `crates/io-gltf/src/cache_stub.rs`: replaced the W17 module-doc comment with
+  the current policy (local typed `Cache` + `MemoryCache` remain the default,
+  `AssetStoreCache` is opt-in). No code, trait signatures, struct fields,
+  impls, derives, tests, or behavior changed.
+- `crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs` (new): defines
+  `pub struct AssetStoreCache` that:
+  - Holds a `Box<dyn rge_asset_store::Cache>` backing plus a typed mirror
+    `HashMap` per asset family (mesh / material / animation / skeleton /
+    image) — required to keep the existing borrowed-`Option<&T>` shape of
+    `crate::Cache`'s `get_*` methods unchanged.
+  - Implements `crate::Cache` by best-effort write-through to the backing
+    plus a typed-mirror insert. The infallible trait contract is preserved
+    (insert returns a handle; get returns the typed asset). Backing errors
+    in the trait path are intentionally discarded — callers that need to
+    observe them use the fallible API.
+  - Provides additive fallible adapters
+    `try_insert_mesh` / `_material` / `_animation` / `_skeleton` / `_image`
+    that return `Result<*Handle, GltfError>` and surface
+    `rge_asset_store::CacheError` as `GltfError::Cache` via a new
+    `impl From<rge_asset_store::CacheError> for GltfError`. The typed
+    mirror is not updated when the backing put fails, so adapter state
+    stays consistent on error.
+  - Exposes static helpers `asset_id_for_{mesh,material,animation,skeleton,image}`
+    that map a typed `*Handle` to the corresponding `rge_asset_store::AssetId`
+    via `AssetId::from_raw(h.0)`. Canonical byte forms below are chosen so
+    `AssetId::from_bytes(canonical_bytes) == AssetId::from_raw(handle.0)`,
+    keeping the asset-store entry and the typed handle byte-for-byte
+    identical.
+  - Exposes `backing(&self) -> &dyn rge_asset_store::Cache` so tests (and
+    callers that need to query / evict the backing directly) can probe
+    the seam without breaking encapsulation.
+  - Constructors: generic `new<C: Cache + 'static>(backing: C)` for
+    concrete impls and `from_box(Box<dyn Cache>)` for dyn-selected impls.
+  - Private free functions `mesh_canonical_bytes`,
+    `material_canonical_bytes`, `animation_canonical_bytes`,
+    `skeleton_canonical_bytes`, `image_canonical_bytes` emit the BLAKE3
+    pre-image used by each asset family's existing `content_hash()`
+    method. Reusing the same byte stream guarantees AssetId/Handle
+    parity.
+  - No new file other than this module and the EXEC report. No edits
+    to existing call sites in editor / loader / importer / exporter /
+    integration-test paths — those continue to use `MemoryCache` by default.
+
+### Tests
+
+All seven tests live in `crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs::tests`:
+
+- `insert_mesh_returns_same_handle_as_memory_cache` — pins parity:
+  `MemoryCache::insert_mesh` and `AssetStoreCache::insert_mesh` (backed
+  by `rge_asset_store::InMemoryCache`) yield the same `MeshHandle` for
+  the same `MeshAsset`, and `get_mesh` returns the original asset.
+- `re_inserting_same_mesh_yields_stable_handle` — pins handle stability:
+  inserting the same mesh twice produces the same `MeshHandle` and the
+  typed mirror's mesh count is exactly 1.
+- `duplicate_inserts_yield_one_asset_store_entry` — pins byte-level
+  dedup through the asset-store backing: after two identical inserts,
+  `adapter.backing().len() == 1`, and `backing().get(asset_id_for_mesh(h))`
+  returns `Some(canonical_bytes)`. Asserts dedup is observed on the
+  asset-store path, not just the typed mirror.
+- `distinct_meshes_get_distinct_asset_ids` — pins content addressing:
+  two meshes differing by a single position component produce different
+  handles and the backing ends up with exactly 2 entries.
+- `try_insert_mesh_surfaces_backing_error_as_gltf_cache` — pins error
+  surface: with a `FailingBacking` test double whose
+  `put`/`get`/`evict_lru` always return `CacheError::Io`,
+  `try_insert_mesh` returns `Err(GltfError::Cache(_))` (not `Schema`,
+  not `Io`, not a panic). Confirms the typed mirror is NOT mutated on
+  backing failure (`adapter.meshes.len() == 0`).
+- `try_insert_image_surfaces_backing_error_as_gltf_cache` — same error
+  surface verified on the image family to confirm the From-impl
+  applies uniformly across families.
+- `cache_error_bad_asset_id_maps_to_gltf_cache_variant` — pins that
+  `CacheError::BadAssetId` (not just `Io`) lands on
+  `GltfError::Cache`, so `From<CacheError>` doesn't leak into another
+  error category.
+
+### Docs
+
+- Module-level doc on the new `asset_store_cache.rs` explains the opt-in
+  policy, shape-mismatch resolution, and error surfacing rules.
+- Updated comment in `cache_stub.rs` per the Planner's doctrine-correction
+  note: the local typed `Cache` + `MemoryCache` remain the public
+  contract; `crate::Cache` is not a re-export of `rge_asset_store::Cache`.
+- No edits to any `docs/`, ADR, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `change.md`,
+  or other handoff packet beyond this EXEC report.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+Not needed — the lists above already cover the scope.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` before edits → empty (tracked
+  files clean; the two `ISSUE-94_TASK_*` packet files were the only
+  untracked items).
+- `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-io-gltf -- --check` → exit 0.
+- `cargo build -p rge-io-gltf` → exit 0. Build emitted no warnings on
+  the final pass (the initial `let _ = backing.put(...)` warnings about
+  `let-underscore-drop` were resolved by switching to `drop(...)`).
+- `cargo test -p rge-io-gltf --lib --no-fail-fast` → exit 0; **49 passed
+  / 0 failed / 0 ignored**. The seven new `asset_store_cache::tests::*`
+  cases are included and pass.
+- `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` → exit 0. All ten
+  enforcement lints PASS, all supplementary lints PASS. Pre-existing
+  warning-level notice `io-* crate rge-io-gltf missing
+  package.metadata.rge.formats declaration` is unchanged by this
+  dispatch (it predates ISSUE-94 and applies to every `rge-io-*` crate);
+  not addressed here because the metadata-key edit is out of allowed
+  scope.
+- `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1`
+  → exit 0; `VERIFY OK: all 6 verification step(s) passed.` (fmt-check,
+  arch-lints, lint-suite tests, build, workspace tests, workspace
+  doctests).
+- `git diff -- Cargo.lock` → exactly one line added: `+ "rge-asset-store",`
+  inside the `rge-io-gltf` package's dependencies list. No new
+  third-party package, version, checksum, source, or unrelated edge.
+  Full diff (12 lines of unified output, of which 1 is the substantive
+  change):
+
+  ```
+  diff --git a/Cargo.lock b/Cargo.lock
+  index d7230e7..5acf7fa 100644
+  --- a/Cargo.lock
+  +++ b/Cargo.lock
+  @@ -4567,6 +4567,7 @@ version = "0.0.1"
+   dependencies = [
+    "blake3",
+    "gltf",
+  + "rge-asset-store",
+    "rge-io-image",
+    "ron",
+    "serde",
+  ```
+
+- `git status --short` after verification → only the scoped files plus
+  this EXEC report and its task-side packet sidecar:
+
+  ```
+   M Cargo.lock
+   M crates/io-gltf/Cargo.toml
+   M crates/io-gltf/src/cache_stub.rs
+   M crates/io-gltf/src/lib.rs
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-94_TASK_2026-05-22_20-20-17+0300.md
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-94_TASK_2026-05-22_20-20-17+0300.meta.json
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-94_EXEC_2026-05-22_20-41-02+0300.md
+  ?? crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs
+  ```
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope.
+
+Notes on judgement calls within scope:
+
+- The TASK says the fallible adapter API surfaces `rge_asset_store::CacheError`
+  as `GltfError::Cache`. I implemented that via
+  `impl From<rge_asset_store::CacheError> for GltfError` inside
+  `asset_store_cache.rs` (the `impl` lives in the adapter module, not on
+  the local `Cache` trait). `GltfError::Cache(String)` is the variant
+  surface used by both the From impl and the fallible adapter methods.
+- The infallible `crate::Cache::insert_*` methods on `AssetStoreCache`
+  attempt the backing `put` and discard its `Result` via `drop(...)`.
+  This was the only way to exercise the asset-store seam from the
+  existing infallible trait shape without panicking or changing the
+  trait. Callers that need to observe backing failures use the
+  `try_insert_*` family — matching the Planner Note that "Asset-store
+  failures cannot be surfaced as `GltfError::Cache` without panics or
+  without changing the local `crate::Cache` trait" is a HALT condition,
+  and was avoided here by routing failure observation through the
+  additive fallible API instead.
+- The canonical byte form for each asset family deliberately mirrors
+  the byte stream hashed inside that asset's existing `content_hash()`
+  method, so `AssetId::from_bytes(canonical_bytes) ==
+  AssetId::from_raw(handle.0)`. This avoids introducing a new
+  serialization format (e.g. bincode), which would have added an
+  unrelated dependency to `Cargo.lock` and tripped the Halt condition
+  on lock-file scope.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+- None. The implementation matches the Task Packet's stated scope and
+  acceptance criteria. If the Planner later wants the editor / loader /
+  importer / exporter call sites to opt into `AssetStoreCache`, that's
+  a separate dispatch — by design this packet does not wire the
+  adapter into any default code path.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: 4 modified — `Cargo.lock`, `crates/io-gltf/Cargo.toml`,
+  `crates/io-gltf/src/cache_stub.rs`, `crates/io-gltf/src/lib.rs`.
+- Untracked items: 4 — `crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs` (the
+  new adapter module), `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-94_TASK_2026-05-22_20-20-17+0300.md`
+  + its `.meta.json` sidecar (the dispatch's TASK packet), and this
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-94_EXEC_2026-05-22_20-41-02+0300.md` report.
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-94`
+- Last commit: `0600dcc chore(dispatch): tighten task #5 verbatim MUSTs to cover all 5 audit Q5 halts`
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-94
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

@@ -0,0 +1,130 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-94
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-22_20-20-17+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs
+- crates/io-gltf/src/lib.rs
+- crates/io-gltf/src/cache_stub.rs
+- crates/io-gltf/Cargo.toml
+- Cargo.lock
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-94_EXEC_*.md
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Implement GitHub issue #94 by adding an opt-in `rge-io-gltf` adapter that lets callers back the existing typed `crate::cache_stub::Cache` surface with `dyn rge_asset_store::Cache` byte storage. This must be an additive adapter inside `rge-io-gltf`: `MemoryCache` remains the default cache used by existing editor, loader, and test paths, and `crate::Cache` remains the local typed trait rather than becoming a re-export of `rge_asset_store::Cache`.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `crates/io-gltf/src/lib.rs`
+- `crates/io-gltf/src/cache_stub.rs`, comment-only. Do not change code, trait methods, structs, impls, derives, tests, or behavior in this file.
+- `crates/io-gltf/Cargo.toml`, only to add the direct `rge-asset-store` dependency required by the adapter.
+- `Cargo.lock`, only for the single dependency edge that records `rge-io-gltf` depending on the existing workspace package `rge-asset-store`.
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-94_EXEC_*.md`, only the execution report required by the handoff protocol.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- `crates/io-gltf/src/cache_stub.rs` code or public trait method signatures.
+- Any existing `crate::Cache` method name, receiver, argument type, or return type, including all `insert_*` and `get_*` methods.
+- Existing `MemoryCache` fields, impls, behavior, or default use sites.
+- `kernel/**`
+- `editor/**`
+- `crates/editor-shell/**`
+- `crates/io-image/**`

+## Deliverables
+
+- A new `crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs` module defining an opt-in adapter type, expected name `AssetStoreCache`, that implements the existing local `crate::cache_stub::Cache` trait while forwarding storage through a caller-supplied `dyn rge_asset_store::Cache`.
+- Additive fallible adapter APIs, if needed because the existing `crate::Cache` trait is intentionally infallible, that surface underlying `rge_asset_store::CacheError` values as `GltfError::Cache`. These APIs must live on the adapter and must not alter `crate::Cache`.
+- `crates/io-gltf/src/lib.rs` wiring for the new module and public adapter type, plus a `GltfError::Cache(String)` variant or equivalent `GltfError::Cache` surface for asset-store failures. Do not replace `pub use cache_stub::{Cache, MemoryCache};`.
+- `crates/io-gltf/Cargo.toml` updated with the minimal direct path dependency on `rge-asset-store`; do not edit root workspace dependencies.
+- `crates/io-gltf/src/cache_stub.rs` comment updated only to stop saying that W16 will delete this file or turn `crate::Cache` into a trait re-export. The comment should state the current policy: local typed `Cache` and `MemoryCache` remain the default, with the asset-store adapter as opt-in.
+- Unit tests in `asset_store_cache.rs` covering:
+  - MemoryCache parity for at least mesh insertion/get behavior and handle stability.
+  - BLAKE3 content-addressed dedup through asset-store bytes, using an asset-store-backed cache path rather than only the local typed mirror.
+  - Underlying asset-store I/O/cache errors surfacing as `GltfError::Cache`, not as panics and not as `GltfError::Schema` or `GltfError::Io`.
+- One `ISSUE-94_EXEC_*.md` execution report documenting files changed, adapter shape, tests added, verification results, any skipped gate, and final worktree state.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- `rge_io_gltf::MemoryCache` remains available, unchanged, and the default type used by all existing editor, loader, importer, exporter, and test call sites unless a caller explicitly opts into the new adapter.
+- `rge_io_gltf::Cache` continues to refer to the existing local typed trait from `cache_stub.rs`; it is not replaced by, aliased to, or re-exported from `rge_asset_store::Cache`.
+- The adapter is defined inside `rge-io-gltf` and bridges to `dyn rge_asset_store::Cache`; no trait or implementation changes are made in `crates/asset-store/**`.
+- The existing public `crate::Cache` method signatures are byte-for-byte semantically preserved: no `Result` return is added, no handle type changes, no owned-return replacement for borrowed `get_*`, and no method removals or renames.
+- The adapter's tests prove parity with `MemoryCache` for the covered asset family: inserting the same asset yields the same typed handle, getting that handle returns equivalent typed asset data, and re-inserting identical content does not create divergent handles.
+- The adapter's tests prove asset-store-backed byte dedup: identical serialized/canonical bytes sent through `rge_asset_store::Cache::put` produce a single content-addressed asset-store entry or the same `AssetId`, and this is asserted through the asset-store backing path.
+- The adapter's tests include a failing asset-store test double or equivalent failing backing cache and assert an `Err(GltfError::Cache(_))` from the adapter's fallible API.
+- `GltfError::Cache` is used only for asset-store cache failures. Existing parse, schema, JSON, and ordinary file I/O error behavior must not be relabeled.
+- `Cargo.lock` changes, if any, are limited to adding `rge-asset-store` to the existing `rge-io-gltf` package dependency list. No new third-party package, version, checksum, source, or unrelated dependency edge is acceptable.
+- The diff is limited to `crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs`, `crates/io-gltf/src/lib.rs`, `crates/io-gltf/src/cache_stub.rs`, `crates/io-gltf/Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, and the required `ISSUE-94_EXEC_*.md` report.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not make asset-store the default cache for io-gltf.
+- Do not wire the new adapter into existing editor, loader, importer, exporter, or integration-test call sites.
+- Do not delete, rename, or shrink `MemoryCache`.
+- Do not re-export `rge_asset_store::Cache` as `rge_io_gltf::Cache`.
+- Do not change the byte-oriented API or implementation of `rge-asset-store`.
+- Do not touch `io-image`, kernel, editor, or editor-shell code as part of this dispatch.
+- Do not add feature flags unless the existing build fails without one and a Planner-approved correction packet permits it.
+- Do not solve broader asset-view, cooker, streaming, or persistent-cache policy questions.
+- Do not commit or push.
+

+- `cargo build -p rge-io-gltf` -> expected exit 0.

+- `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` -> expected exit 0.

+- `git status --short` after verification -> expected changed files limited to the scoped source/manifest/lock files plus the required `ISSUE-94_EXEC_*.md` report.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without committing and request fresh Planner guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- Implementing the adapter appears to require changing any public `crate::Cache` `insert_*` or `get_*` method signature.
+- Implementing the adapter appears to require replacing `MemoryCache`, changing existing default call sites, or routing editor/loaders/tests through asset-store by default.
+- Implementing the adapter appears to require edits outside the allowed files, especially under `kernel/**`, `editor/**`, `crates/editor-shell/**`, `crates/io-image/**`, or `crates/asset-store/**`.
+- A working implementation requires a trait re-export instead of an opt-in adapter type inside `rge-io-gltf`.
+- Asset-store failures cannot be surfaced as `GltfError::Cache` without panics or without changing the local `crate::Cache` trait.
+- The `Cargo.lock` diff extends beyond the single new `rge-asset-store` dependency edge for `rge-io-gltf`.
+- Architecture lints fail because the new `rge-io-gltf -> rge-asset-store` dependency edge is forbidden. Do not edit lint rules in this dispatch.
+- Any required verification gate fails for a reason outside the scoped files and cannot be resolved within the allowed scope.
+- The worktree contains unrelated edits in any allowed file that make a precise scoped diff ambiguous.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+Issue #92 established that `io-gltf`'s typed cache shape is not the same as `rge_asset_store::Cache`: `io-gltf` stores typed assets and returns borrowed `Option<&T>`, while asset-store stores fallible byte blobs and returns owned bytes. This dispatch intentionally avoids a wholesale replacement. A small typed mirror inside the adapter is acceptable if needed to satisfy the existing borrowed `get_*` lifetime shape, but the adapter must still exercise the asset-store backing path and tests must prove byte-level dedup/errors through that backing path.
+
+Use the existing asset-family content-hash behavior as the compatibility anchor. Keep the bridge local to `asset_store_cache.rs`; avoid spreading serialization or cache policy into importer/exporter modules. The `cache_stub.rs` edit is only a doctrine/comment correction: the local typed trait remains the public contract for io-gltf, and the new asset-store bridge is an explicit opt-in for callers that want persistent byte backing.
+
+Under the orchestrator protocol, no `_REVIEW_` or `_CLOSEOUT_` packet is expected from this TASK. The Executor should produce one `ISSUE-94_EXEC_*.md` report for this run.
